title: The Well-Grounded Rubyist
subtitle: Ruby Programming from the Ground Up
author: David A. Black, Joseph Leo III
themes: Ruby's object model: everything is an object, the method-lookup path, singleton classes
Blocks, procs, and lambdas: closures, binding, and functional techniques in Ruby
Modules and mixins: namespace organization, multiple inheritance alternative
Metaprogramming and reflection: define_method, send, method_missing
Duck typing over class hierarchy: programming to behavior, not type
Standard library as a first-class resource: file I/O, regex, enumerators
Threading and concurrency: fibers, Mutex, race conditions
Testing with RSpec: BDD workflow and expressive specifications
awards: Best-selling Ruby tutorial with over 4 editions
The definitive intermediate-to-advanced Ruby book from Manning
Praise cited by hundreds of Ruby developers as the book that made them understand Ruby deeply
excerpt: The Well-Grounded Rubyist is a beautifully written tutorial that begins with your first Ruby program and takes you all the way to sophisticated topics like reflection, threading, and recursion. It is not a dry syntax reference — it teaches the principles that make Ruby unique: everything is an object, blocks are first-class citizens, and the object model is accessible enough to use and manipulate at runtime.
